description_si: Vite UI + Node API (travel-roi bot) — full source snapshot embedded for backup / portability.

repo.render.yaml:
# Render Blueprint — https://render.com/docs/blueprint-spec
# • Dashboard → Web Service: Build `npm ci --omit=dev` | Start `npm start` (if not using Blueprint).
# • `omit=dev` = API-only install (no Vite/vitest) — faster, fewer OOM failures on small builders.
# • Do NOT use `npm run dev:all` — Vite fails on Render.
#
# Clone failed: "Could not resolve host: github.com" — Render’s builder could not DNS-resolve GitHub (transient
# outage or regional DNS). Not fixable in app code. Try: Manual Deploy again, wait and retry, check
# https://www.githubstatus.com , or reconnect the GitHub repo under Service → Settings.

services:
  - type: web
    name: facebook-marketing-app
    runtime: node
    buildCommand: npm ci --omit=dev
    startCommand: npm start
    healthCheckPath: /
    envVars:
      - key: NODE_ENV
        value: production

repo.public/privacy/index.html:
Privacy Policy

Last updated: March 2026. This policy describes how we handle information for this application.

Who we are

This policy applies to the Facebook Marketing / Bot Ops web application (the “App”) used to manage advertising workflows, campaign tooling, and related analytics for authorized business use.

What the App does

The App provides a console for operations such as viewing dashboard state, managing campaign and creative workflows, audience and CRM-related features, automation, and—when you configure it—calls to the Meta Marketing API. The exact features available depend on your deployment and whether you use live Meta credentials or mock / test mode.

Meta Platform Terms

Where this App uses Meta products (for example the Marketing API, ad accounts, or related permissions), we use those products in line with the Meta Platform Terms, Developer Policies, and other applicable Meta terms and product policies. Nothing in this policy limits Meta’s own terms or your obligations when using Meta.

Information we collect or process

We collect or process information only as needed to run the App. This may include data you provide directly, data we receive from Meta when you authorize access (subject to the permissions and tokens you grant), and basic technical data as described below.

  • Account and access data — for example API keys or access tokens you configure (stored according to your hosting environment), identifiers needed to authenticate requests, and basic technical logs (such as timestamps and error messages).
  • Data from Meta (when you connect Meta) — depending on your permissions and use of the App, this may include identifiers and metadata related to your Meta ad account, campaigns, ad sets, ads, creatives, insights or performance metrics, audience or custom audience operations, page or asset identifiers, and other fields returned by the Meta APIs you enable. Meta processes much of this on Meta’s systems; we process what our App and backend store or display to provide the service.
  • Advertising, CRM, and operational data — such as campaign names, ad set settings, creative copy or asset references, funnel or CRM-related fields, and similar data you enter or sync through the App.
  • Technical and usage data — for example server or application logs, IP addresses, and device or browser information as typically logged by hosting infrastructure, to secure and operate the service.
  • Support communications — information you send when you contact us (for example by email).

We do not sell personal data. We do not use Meta user data for purposes prohibited by Meta’s policies.

How we use information (purposes)

We use information only for purposes such as:

  • Providing, maintaining, and improving the App and its features;
  • Displaying dashboards, analytics, and reporting you request;
  • Communicating with you about support, security, or policy updates;
  • Responding to privacy rights and data-deletion requests;
  • Complying with law and enforcing our terms.

Meta and other third parties

When you connect Meta, Meta also processes data under Meta’s own policies and terms. We do not control Meta’s systems. See Meta’s resources for how Meta handles account, advertising, and related data. Other third-party services you connect may have their own terms.

How to request deletion of your data

Meta expects developers to offer a clear way for people to request deletion of data the App has obtained from or through Meta or the developer. You may:

  • Email us at kanaayams@gmail.com with a data deletion request; or
  • Follow the instructions on our dedicated page: Data deletion instructions.
  • If you connected this App using Facebook or Meta login, you can also review or remove the App from your Facebook/Meta account settings (for example under Apps and websites or connected experiences, as Meta describes in its Help Center). Removing the App there may not delete all data we already hold; use email or the data deletion page if you need a full deletion from our systems.

Retention

We keep information only as long as needed for the purposes above or as required by law. Retention depends on your backend storage (for example SQLite or hosting logs). You may request deletion as described on our Data deletion page.

Security

We take reasonable measures to protect data in line with the nature of the service. No method of transmission or storage is 100% secure; use strong credentials and follow Meta’s security recommendations for tokens and ad accounts.

Your choices and rights

Depending on your location, you may have rights to access, correct, or delete personal data, or to object to certain processing. To request deletion, use the options in How to request deletion of your data above. For other requests or questions, contact us at the email below.

Children

The App is intended for business users and is not directed at children under 16.

Changes

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will post the updated version on this page and revise the “Last updated” date when we do.

Contact

Questions, privacy requests, or deletion: kanaayams@gmail.com

Related: Terms of Service · Data deletion
← Back to app
repo.public/terms/index.html:
Terms of Service

Last updated: March 2026. These terms govern use of this application (the “Service”) — the Bot Ops console for advertising workflows, analytics, and related tools.

Agreement

By accessing or using the Service, you agree to these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, do not use the Service.

Meta Platform Terms and policies

If you use Meta products through this Service (including the Meta Marketing API, ad accounts, or related features), you must comply with the Meta Platform Terms, Developer Policies, Community Standards, and Advertising Standards (and any other applicable Meta product terms). If there is a conflict between these Terms and Meta’s required terms for platform use, Meta’s platform terms apply to your use of Meta products.

The Service

The Service helps authorized businesses manage advertising-related workflows: for example dashboard views, campaign and creative tooling, audience and CRM-related features, automation hooks, and integrations with the Meta Marketing API when you configure valid credentials. Features depend on your environment (including mock vs live Meta mode) and server configuration.

The Service is provided for lawful business use only. You are responsible for your own ad content, targeting, spend, and compliance with applicable laws and platform rules.

Your responsibilities

  • Keep API keys, access tokens, and account credentials secure and do not share them inappropriately.
  • Comply with Meta’s terms, Advertising Standards, and other applicable Meta policies when using Meta features.
  • Do not use the Service to violate law, infringe others’ rights, or send deceptive or harmful ads.
  • Provide accurate information when contacting support or requesting data deletion.

Acceptable use

You may not probe, disrupt, or overload the Service; scrape it in a way that harms performance; or attempt unauthorized access to systems or data. We may suspend access if we reasonably believe these terms or the law are violated.

Intellectual property

The Service, its branding, and its original content are owned by us or our licensors. Meta trademarks and products belong to Meta. You retain rights to your own ad creative and business content you upload or configure.

Third-party services

The Service may call Meta’s APIs and other third-party systems. Those services are operated by their respective providers under their own terms. We do not control Meta’s availability, pricing, or policy changes.

Disclaimers

The Service is provided “as is” and “as available.” We do not guarantee uninterrupted operation, error-free behavior, or specific advertising outcomes (including ROAS, leads, or delivery).

Limitation of liability

To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, we are not liable for any indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages, or for lost profits or data, arising from your use of the Service. Your sole remedy for dissatisfaction with the Service is to stop using it.

Termination

You may stop using the Service at any time. We may suspend or discontinue the Service or your access where reasonably necessary for legal, security, or operational reasons. Provisions that by nature should survive (including disclaimers, liability limits, and governing law) will survive termination.

Changes

We may update these terms from time to time. Continued use of the Service after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated terms. Material changes may be noted on this page or via the contact email below.

Governing law

These terms are governed by applicable law without regard to conflict-of-law rules, except where mandatory consumer protections in your country cannot be waived.

Contact

Questions about these terms: kanaayams@gmail.com

Related: Privacy Policy · Data deletion
← Back to app
repo.public/data-deletion/index.html:
Data Deletion Instructions

How to request removal of data associated with this application.

Overview

You can ask us to delete or stop using personal data processed through this App (subject to legal or technical limits—for example data we must keep for security or compliance, or data held only by Meta on Meta’s systems).

Through your Facebook or Meta account

If you granted this App access via Facebook or Meta, you can review or remove the App from your account using Meta’s settings (for example Settings & privacy → Settings → Apps and websites on Facebook, or the equivalent in Accounts Center). Meta explains how in its Help Center. Removing the App may stop future data sharing; it does not always delete data we already received. For deletion from our systems, use email or the steps below.

How to request deletion from us

We process data deletion requests promptly, in line with our obligations under the Meta Platform Terms and applicable law. Send an email to:

kanaayams@gmail.com

Use the subject line “Data deletion request” so we can route your message quickly.

What to include

Please include enough detail for us to find your data, such as:

  • The email address or account identifier you use with the App or with our team;
  • Your business or project name as shown in the App (if applicable);
  • A clear statement that you want your data deleted or that you withdraw consent for processing;
  • Any relevant dates or screenshots (optional) if you are following up on a specific issue.

What we will do

After we receive your request we will:

  • Confirm receipt where appropriate;
  • Verify that the request relates to data we control in this App or our systems;
  • Delete or anonymize that data without undue delay where possible, or explain any limitation (for example data held only inside Meta must be addressed using Meta’s tools or policies);
  • Respond within a reasonable time, typically within 30 days for straightforward requests, unless a longer period is required by volume or complexity.

If this App is configured with Meta’s Data Deletion Request callback URL in the Meta App Dashboard, Meta may also notify our server when a user removes the App; we still honor email requests sent to the address above.

Meta and ad accounts

Campaigns, ads, and audiences stored in Meta are subject to Meta’s tools and policies. Deleting data in our App does not automatically delete your Meta ad account or objects inside Meta; you may need to use Meta Business settings for that.

Related

For how we handle data in general, see our Privacy Policy. For rules of use, see Terms of Service.

Related: Privacy Policy · Terms of Service
← Back to app
repo.docs/backend-blueprint.md:
# Travel ROI Meta Bot — Backend Blueprint

Sinhala: මෙය backend එකේ **schema**, **API surface**, සහ **data flow** සාරාංශයයි.

---

## 1. High-level architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  subgraph client [Client / Dashboard]
    UI[Vite UI :5173]
  end
  subgraph api [HTTP API server/index.js]
    Routes[Route handlers]
    RL[Rate limit POST]
    Auth[X-API-Key optional]
  end
  subgraph core [Domain]
    State[state.js in-memory]
    Engine[engine-store.js]
    Loop[loop-engine.js]
    Orch[orchestrator.js]
    Meta[meta-graph.js Marketing API]
    Aud[meta-audience.js Custom Audiences]
    CAPI[meta-capi.js Conversions API]
  end
  subgraph data [Persistence]
    SQLite[(SQLite bot-engine.db)]
    Legacy[[bot-state.json legacy import]]
  end
  UI --> Routes
  Routes --> RL --> Auth
  Routes --> State
  Routes --> Engine
  Engine --> SQLite
  Legacy -.one-time migrate.-> SQLite
  Loop --> Meta
  Loop --> Engine
  Orch --> Meta
  Aud --> Meta
  CAPI --> Meta
  CAPI --> SQLite
```

---

## 2. SQLite schema (engine DB)

**File:** `data/bot-engine.db` (or `ENGINE_DB_PATH`).  
**Migration:** version `1` in `server/db/sqlite.js`.

| Table | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `schema_migrations` | Applied migration versions |
| `engine_funnel` | Single row (`id=1`): funnel counters — `view_content`, `lead_count`, `qualified`, `booking` |
| `engine_settings` | Single row (`id=1`): `tracking_health` |
| `engine_jobs` | Ad pipeline / job JSON blobs (`job_json`), capped ~200 |
| `engine_decisions` | Loop / decision log (`decision_json`), capped ~300 |
| `engine_crm_log` | CRM append log (`log_json`), capped ~500 |
| `engine_meta_campaigns` | PK `(campaign_name, adset_name)` → Meta IDs for hydration |
| `engine_performance` | PK `meta_adset_id` — spend, funnel metrics, `revenue`, `cogs`, `currency` |
| `engine_revenue_events` | PK `order_id` — idempotent revenue rows (`event_json`) |
| `engine_crm_quality` | Lead quality events (`event_json`) |
| `engine_capi_log` | CAPI send proof: `event_id`, `event_name`, `pixel_id`, `http_status`, `response_json` |
| `engine_scale_history` | PK `meta_adset_id` — last scale time + budget (minor units) |
| `engine_audience_sync` | Audience sync audit (`segment`, `audience_id`, `matched_users`, `detail_json`) |
| `meta_targeting_catalog` | Cached Graph `targetingsearch` rows (`meta_id`, `search_type`, `search_q`, …) |

**Legacy:** If DB is empty and `data/bot-state.json` exists, `initEngineStore()` imports it once.

---

## 3. HTTP API surface

Base: `http://localhost:${API_PORT}` (default **3001**).

**Auth:** If `API_KEY` is set, all **POST** routes require header `X-API-Key: <API_KEY>`.  
**Rate limit:** POSTs per IP per minute — `RATE_LIMIT_MAX_PER_MIN` (default 120).  
**CORS:** `CORS_ORIGINS` allowlist.

| Method | Path | Notes |
|--------|------|--------|
| GET | `/api/dashboard/state` | Full dashboard payload (in-memory `state` + engine snapshot) |
| GET | `/api/meta/health` | Meta config / mode |
| GET | `/api/meta/targeting-search` | Query: `q`, `type` (e.g. adinterest), `limit` — Graph `targetingsearch`; live caches in `meta_targeting_catalog` |
| POST | `/api/meta/track` | In-memory tracking + funnel bump |
| POST | `/api/pipeline/run` | Full ad pipeline (orchestrator) |
| POST | `/api/preview/render` | Ad preview |
| POST | `/api/loop/tick` | Optimizer loop tick |
| GET | `/api/content/video-script` | Query: `persona`, `destination`, `offer` |
| GET | `/api/policy` | Publish policy |
| GET | `/api/business/summary` | Aggregated business KPIs (incl. `cogs`, `grossProfit`, `profitRoas`) |
| POST | `/api/meta/upload-image` | Graph ad image |
| POST | `/api/meta/upload-video` | Graph video |
| POST | `/api/meta/create-creative` | Link / carousel / video creative |
| POST | `/api/meta/create-campaign` | Campaign chain (live or mock) |
| POST | `/api/meta/sync-audience` | Live: `emails[]` → Custom Audience + hash upload; mock without emails |
| POST | `/api/meta/capi/event` | Server-side CAPI event (logged to `engine_capi_log`) |
| GET | `/api/meta/capi/log` | Query: `limit` — recent CAPI log rows |
| GET | `/api/meta/insights` | Ad account insights + reconcile |
| POST | `/api/meta/optimize` | Same as loop tick |
| POST | `/api/crm/webhook` | CRM events; optional `CRM_WEBHOOK_SECRET` + `X-Webhook-Signature: sha256=...` |
| POST | `/api/crm/quality` | Lead quality → performance |
| POST | `/api/revenue/record` | Revenue (+ optional `cogs`) — idempotent by `orderId` |

---

## 4. External Meta surfaces

| Surface | Module | Env / IDs |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| Marketing API (Graph) | `meta-graph.js` | `META_ACCESS_TOKEN`, `META_AD_ACCOUNT_ID`, `META_PAGE_ID`, `META_PIXEL_ID`, `META_API_VERSION` |
| Custom Audiences | `meta-audience.js` | Same token + ad account |
| Conversions API | `meta-capi.js` | Pixel ID + token (POST `/{pixel-id}/events`) |

---

## 5. Loop / scaling (safe scale)

**Env:** `LOOP_APPLY_META`, `LOOP_APPLY_SCALE`, `LOOP_SCALE_MIN_MINOR`, `LOOP_SCALE_MAX_MINOR`, `LOOP_SCALE_PERCENT`, `LOOP_SCALE_COOLDOWN_MS`.

**Flow:** `loop-engine.js` reads actions from `state.optimizerActions` → optional Graph pause/scale → `engine_decisions` + scale history in SQLite.

---

## 6. Margin-aware optimizer

**Env:** `MARGIN_AWARE_OPTIMIZER`, `OPTIMIZER_CPA_MAX`, `OPTIMIZER_ROAS_MIN`, `OPTIMIZER_PROFIT_ROAS_MIN`, `OPTIMIZER_MIN_LEADS_SCALE`.

**Logic:** `state.js` `recalculateActions()` — when margin mode and COGS present, scale decision uses profit ROAS vs revenue ROAS.

---

## 7. Module map (server)

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `index.js` | HTTP server, routing, CORS, rate limit, webhook verify |
| `state.js` | In-memory demo/dashboard state + `recalculateActions` |
| `engine-store.js` | SQLite persistence API used by routes + loop |
| `db/sqlite.js` | DB open, WAL, migrations, CAPI log helpers |
| `meta-graph.js` | Graph form GET/POST helpers |
| `meta-audience.js` | Audience create + hashed email upload |
| `meta-capi.js` | CAPI send + log |
| `loop-engine.js` | Loop tick + Meta pause/scale |
| `orchestrator.js` | Pipeline jobs, idempotency |
| `insights-sync.js` | Insights → campaign rows |
| `policy.js` | Publish gating |
| `scheduler.js` | Cron |
| `scaling-config.js` | Budget clamps |
| `rate-limit.js` | In-memory rate limit |
| `webhook-verify.js` | CRM HMAC |

---

## 8. Tests

- `npm run test` — Vitest (`server/*.test.js`).

---

*Generated to match the codebase under `server/` and `docs/`.*
repo.README.md:
# Facebook Marketing app

Vite frontend + Node.js API for Meta Marketing API workflows (campaigns, creatives, targeting search, CAPI, CRM hooks).

## Requirements

- Node.js 22+
- Meta app with Marketing API permissions (for live mode)

## Setup

```bash
npm install
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env — add META_ACCESS_TOKEN, META_AD_ACCOUNT_ID (act_…), META_PAGE_ID, etc.
```

## Dev

```bash
# Terminal 1 — API (default http://localhost:3001)
npm run dev:api

# Terminal 2 — UI (default http://localhost:5173)
npm run dev
```

Or both: `npm run dev:all` (recommended — UI + API together).

If the API says a port is busy, it will try the next port in **development**; set **`VITE_API_BASE`** in `.env` to `http://localhost:<that port>` so the UI can reach it.

## Build

```bash
npm run build
npm run preview   # optional: preview production build
```

## Tests

```bash
npm test
```

## Deploy API (e.g. Render)

- **Build command:** `npm install`
- **Start command:** `npm start` (or `npm run dev:api`)
- **Port:** the server listens on **`PORT`** (Render injects this) or **`API_PORT`** / `3001` locally.
- **Environment:** set **`META_*`** and other vars from `.env.example`. Add **`CORS_ORIGINS`** with your Vercel app URL (e.g. `https://your-app.vercel.app`) so the browser can call the API.
- **`API_KEY`:** recommended in production. On Render, the app boots without it but logs a warning; set `API_KEY` in the Render dashboard when ready.

**Vercel (UI):** set **`VITE_API_BASE`** to your Render service URL (`https://…onrender.com`), then redeploy.

## Security

- Never commit `.env` (gitignored). Copy from `.env.example` and fill secrets locally.
- Rotate Meta tokens if they were ever exposed.

## Push to GitHub

If this repo is not linked yet, add your **GitHub username** and remote, then push:

```bash
git remote add origin https://github.com/YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME/Facebook-Marketing-app.git
git branch -M main
git push -u origin main
```

If `origin` already exists with a wrong URL:

```bash
git remote set-url origin https://github.com/YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME/Facebook-Marketing-app.git
git push -u origin main
```

Use a [Personal Access Token](https://github.com/settings/tokens) as the password when Git asks (HTTPS), or use SSH: `git@github.com:YOUR_USERNAME/Facebook-Marketing-app.git`.
repo..env.example:
# Meta Marketing API (live mode when META_ACCESS_TOKEN + META_AD_ACCOUNT_ID are set)
# After editing tokens: restart the API. If you still see "Session expired", (1) kill the old process on API_PORT,
# (2) see server/env-bootstrap.js — in dev, .env overrides a stale META_ACCESS_TOKEN exported in your shell.
META_API_VERSION=v23.0
META_ACCESS_TOKEN=your_meta_long_lived_access_token
META_AD_ACCOUNT_ID=act_xxxxxxxxxxxx
META_PAGE_ID=your_page_id
META_PIXEL_ID=your_pixel_id

# Meta App Dashboard → Settings → Basic (server-only; never commit real secrets)
META_APP_ID=
META_APP_SECRET=

API_PORT=3001

# Instagram professional account ID (Graph) — link Page↔IG in Business Manager, then use the IG account id for IG delivery on creatives
# META_INSTAGRAM_ACTOR_ID=

# Ad set placements: fb_ig_mobile (default) | fb_ig_all_devices | auto | none — or set META_PLACEMENT_TARGETING_JSON for full control
# META_PLACEMENT_PRESET=fb_ig_mobile
# META_PLACEMENT_TARGETING_JSON=

# Enforce feed-friendly copy limits on link ads (headline 40 / description 30 chars). Set true to allow longer copy
# META_SKIP_CREATIVE_COPY_VALIDATION=false

# Campaign defaults (aligned with qualified_booking_roas — sales / conversions, not raw traffic)
# META_DEFAULT_OBJECTIVE=OUTCOME_SALES
# META_DEFAULT_OPTIMIZATION_GOAL=OFFSITE_CONVERSIONS
# META_CUSTOM_EVENT_TYPE=PURCHASE

APP_BASE_URL=http://localhost:5173

# --- Vite UI (prefix VITE_) ---
# Local API: VITE_API_BASE=http://localhost:3001 (use with npm run dev:all; match API_PORT if dev:api falls back to 3002+)
# Remote API (e.g. Render): VITE_API_BASE=https://your-app.onrender.com — run npm run dev only; set CORS on the host:
# CORS_ORIGINS=http://localhost:5173,http://localhost:5174
# VITE_API_BASE=http://localhost:3001
# VITE_API_KEY=change-me
# VITE_AUDIENCE_SYNC_EMAILS=you@example.com,other@example.com

# Dashboard seed data: demo rows (default). Set false for production (empty campaigns until Meta/CRM).
# SEED_DEMO_DATA=false

# When set to "false", POST /api/meta/track does not also send Conversions API (CAPI) events (funnel still updates).
# Default: track forwards to CAPI in live mode when META_PIXEL_ID is set (same eventId for dedup).
# TRACK_SYNC_CAPI=false

# Advantage Campaign Budget (recommended): META_CBO=true — budget on the campaign (+ bid_strategy); ad sets have no daily_budget.
# Single-ad-set: campaign daily_budget = request dailyBudget. Multi (2–4): campaign daily_budget = dailyBudget × ad set count.
# META_CBO=false → legacy ABO (per–ad-set daily_budget + is_adset_budget_sharing_enabled). Does not change OUTCOME_SALES / optimization goal.
META_CBO=true

# With CBO=true, ad set omits daily_budget and bid_strategy (campaign has budget + bid); verify step re-applies campaign daily_budget if Graph drops it on create.
# LOOP_APPLY_SCALE + CBO: budget change targets the campaign (not the ad set).

# When META_CBO=false (ABO), Graph requires is_adset_budget_sharing_enabled on each ad set with daily_budget.
# META_ADSET_BUDGET_SHARING=false

# Optional: campaign bid strategy when META_CBO=true (default LOWEST_COST_WITHOUT_CAP). Use "omit" if Graph rejects campaign create.
# META_CAMPAIGN_BID_STRATEGY=LOWEST_COST_WITHOUT_CAP

# Meta daily_budget minor units: 2 = cents (USD), 0 = whole yen (JPY)
# META_CURRENCY_MINOR_EXPONENT=2

# Optimizer: min impressions before SCALE when require_statistical_significance (live adsets only)
# OPTIMIZER_MIN_IMPRESSIONS=3000

# Production: server exits if API_KEY missing unless explicitly allowed (dangerous)
# On Render, RENDER=true allows boot without API_KEY (warning logged); still set API_KEY for real traffic.
# ALLOW_UNAUTHENTICATED_POSTS=true

# Render deploy: `render.yaml` — build `npm ci --omit=dev`, start `npm start`. Never `npm run dev:all`.
# If deploy fails before build with "Could not resolve host: github.com" — DNS/network on Render→GitHub; retry Manual Deploy or reconnect repo (not an env/code fix).
# API server port (Render sets PORT automatically; do not set API_PORT there)
API_PORT=3001
# Optional: force bind host (Render uses 0.0.0.0 automatically when RENDER=true)
# BIND_HOST=0.0.0.0
# Max accepted JSON payload bytes (default 1MB)
# MAX_BODY_BYTES=1048576

# Optional write protection for POST routes (recommended outside local dev)
# API_KEY=change-me

# CORS allowlist (comma-separated)
# CORS_ORIGINS=http://localhost:5173,http://localhost:5174

# Durable SQLite path (engine state, CAPI log, scale cooldown)
# ENGINE_DB_PATH=data/bot-engine.db

# CRM webhook HMAC (optional). When set, POST /api/crm/webhook requires header:
# X-Webhook-Signature: sha256=<hex>
# CRM_WEBHOOK_SECRET=

# Rate limit (POST requests per IP per minute)
# RATE_LIMIT_MAX_PER_MIN=120

# Conversions API: POST /api/meta/capi/event (see meta-capi.js). Requires META_PIXEL_ID.

# Live Custom Audience sync: POST /api/meta/sync-audience with JSON { emails: ["a@b.com"], segment, audienceName? }
# Requires Marketing API permissions + audience.

# Safe scaling (minor units = Meta daily_budget units)
# LOOP_SCALE_MIN_MINOR=100
# LOOP_SCALE_MAX_MINOR=50000000
# LOOP_SCALE_PERCENT=20
# LOOP_SCALE_COOLDOWN_MS=86400000

# Margin-aware optimizer (uses profit ROAS when COGS is recorded on performance rows)
# MARGIN_AWARE_OPTIMIZER=false
# OPTIMIZER_CPA_MAX=6
# OPTIMIZER_ROAS_MIN=2.5
# OPTIMIZER_PROFIT_ROAS_MIN=1.2
# OPTIMIZER_MIN_LEADS_SCALE=20

# Waste + qualified funnel (needs CRM quality webhooks into engine_performance.qualified_leads)
# OPTIMIZER_WASTE_SPEND_NO_LEAD=12
# OPTIMIZER_CPA_PAUSE_MIN_SPEND=6
# OPTIMIZER_USE_QUALIFIED_CPA=false
# OPTIMIZER_USE_QUALIFIED_METRICS=false
# OPTIMIZER_MIN_QUALIFIED_RATE=0.12
# OPTIMIZER_MIN_LEADS_FOR_QUALIFIED_CHECK=15
# OPTIMIZER_NO_QUALIFIED_PAUSE_LEADS=12
# OPTIMIZER_NO_QUALIFIED_PAUSE_SPEND=45
# OPTIMIZER_MIN_QUALIFIED_LEADS_SCALE=0

# POST /api/meta/track: pass eventId (match Pixel), optional fbp/fbc/clientUserAgent/clientIpAddress for CAPI match quality

# POST /api/meta/create-campaign & pipeline: optional JSON array `adsets` (max 4) — each { name, targeting }.
# Same campaign + one creative; one ad set + ad per entry. dailyBudget = per ad set. Omit adsets for single-ad-set (legacy).

# When true + live Meta, loop tick PAUSE actions call Marketing API (requires metaAdsetId on row)
# LOOP_APPLY_META=false
# When true + live Meta, SCALE actions increase adset daily budget by 20%
# LOOP_APPLY_SCALE=false

# Insights age: dashboard stale flag + optional block on SCALE when insights not refreshed (GET /api/meta/insights)
# OPTIMIZER_MAX_INSIGHTS_AGE_MS=21600000
# OPTIMIZER_BLOCK_SCALE_STALE_INSIGHTS=false

# Multi–ad-set split: warn when ROAS lags best sibling (dashboard split compare)
# OPTIMIZER_SPLIT_WARN_ROAS_GAP=0.5
# Optional auto-PAUSE clear losers (same Meta campaign id); default off
# OPTIMIZER_SPLIT_AUTO_PAUSE=false
# OPTIMIZER_SPLIT_PAUSE_ROAS_GAP=0.6
# OPTIMIZER_SPLIT_PAUSE_MAX_ROAS=2

# Operator POST routes: /api/meta/adset/status, /budget, /copy, /api/meta/ad/creative — require API_KEY when set

# Cron loop: analyze → decide → act (default every 5 min). Set CRON_ENABLE=false to disable.
# CRON_SCHEDULE=*/5 * * * *
# CRON_ENABLE=true

# Auto-activate ads after pipeline (live only). Mock mode ignores META_AUTO_PUBLISH.
# META_AUTO_PUBLISH=false

# Test vs live: mock if META_USE_MOCK=true OR token/ad account missing (see GET /api/meta/health modeHints).
# Set to "true" to force mock Meta responses even if tokens are set (local UI testing)
# META_USE_MOCK=true

# Targeting search: when Graph omits audience_size, call /reachestimate per row (extra API calls; capped).
# TARGETING_REACH_ESTIMATE=false
# TARGETING_REACH_ESTIMATE_MAX=18

# Debug: GET /api/meta/token-status — Meta debug_token (valid + expiry). Requires META_APP_ID + META_APP_SECRET.
# (Path may include ?query= or a trailing slash; the server normalizes before routing.)
